ogTitle: Scrubby Mountain vs Sedgeford: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Scrubby Mountain and Sedgeford.
heading: Scrubby Mountain vs Sedgeford — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Scrubby Mountain, Queensland and Sedgeford, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sedgeford has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Scrubby Mountain (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Scrubby Mountain or Sedgeford safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Scrubby Mountain has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Sedgeford scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Scrubby Mountain scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Sedgeford scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Scrubby Mountain or Sedgeford?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sedgeford has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
